message:
Fix OOM/DoS vulnerabilities from unbounded length-prefix allocations

Add bounds checks before all memory allocations driven by untrusted
length prefixes read from trace files and network streams. A malicious
or corrupt input could previously force multi-GiB allocations that
crash the process.

Trace file parser (src/trace/file.rs):
- Validate meta_len against MAX_META_LEN (1 MiB) before allocation
- Validate per-event length against MAX_EVENT_LEN (16 MiB)
- Validate compressed chunk length against MAX_COMPRESSED_CHUNK_LEN (64 MiB)
- Cap load_all() pre-allocation to MAX_EVENT_PREALLOC (10M entries)
- Add OversizedField error variant with field name, actual, and max

PostgreSQL wire parser (src/database/postgres.rs):
- Reduce MAX_MESSAGE_LEN from 1 GiB to 64 MiB — sufficient for all
  legitimate messages while preventing single-header DoS

Fixes #8, fixes #10.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
